preamble: The transformation from a voiceless alveolar lateral fricative to an alveolar flap typically occurs through a process of sonorization and loss of lateral friction, often merging with existing liquid phonemes.

examples.note: Reconstructed shift where the voiceless lateral fricative merged with the rhotic flap in daughter languages like Shawnee.
Phonological relationship between voiceless laterals and rhotics, especially evident in the mutation system where both sonorize.

sources: Goddard, I. (1994). The West-to-East Gradient in Algonquian Dialectology.
Ball, M. J., & Müller, N. (2009). The Celtic Languages.
